Brand Architecture AuditAyurvedic Wellness — Women in the Second Half of Life
Kyra Aulani · kyraaulani.art

Category

Ayurveda · Yoga · Holistic Health Coaching

Revenue Stage

Established · Signature Cohort Enrolling

Primary Constraint

A Strong Funnel, Hidden By A Cluttered Homepage

↳ What's solid: a specific niche (Ayurveda for women in the second half of life), a real content engine (Dosha Quiz, podcast, recipes, blog), an active community, and a signature program already enrolling.

↳ The honest tension: the homepage repeats itself, juggles five competing calls to action, and shows no proof — so the funnel you built doesn't convert as hard as it should.

Time-sensitive

Your Vitality Circle Open House is July 22 and enrollment is open now. This audit is organized around sharpening the path to that one next step — before the cohort fills, not after.

Brand Foundation

01

Positioning /
Clarity

✓
Working

Your empathy is exact. "Feeling good should not be a struggle," "tired of piecing information together from multiple sources," "you long for one person who can support you as a whole being" — that's your client's inner monologue, word for word. And the niche is genuinely underserved: Ayurveda for women in the second half of life. You're not competing with every wellness coach; you're speaking to one specific woman at one specific season.

!
Unclear

The homepage says it twice — the hero content is duplicated — and "second half of life," while lovely, is abstract. The woman searching at 11pm is typing "why can't I sleep through menopause" and "Ayurveda for hormonal weight gain," not "second half of life." The page communicates the feeling beautifully but withholds the concrete outcome she's actually looking for.

Your positioning line — use this

Heartfelt Wellbeing is personalized Ayurvedic guidance for women over 40 who are done piecing together health advice — one trusted guide for energy, digestion, sleep, and ease through menopause and beyond.

Brand Presence

The wisdom is there. The proof isn't.

The brand feels warm, calm, and credible — exactly right for this audience. The gap isn't tone; it's repetition and proof. The hero blocks duplicate, several calls to action compete for the same click, and nowhere on the homepage is there a testimonial, a credential, or a student result. For a practice selling invisible, internal transformation, visible proof is the missing layer that turns interest into enrollment.

[ website screenshot ]
✓

Voice: warm and knowing

The pain points read like the client's own thoughts. That empathy is your strongest asset and it's landing.

!

Homepage: repeats and competes

The hero statement appears twice, and Clarity Call, Open House, Signature Program, Free Trial, and email signup all ask for attention at once. No single "start here."

!

Proof is absent

No testimonials, credentials, or outcomes on the homepage. The one thing a hesitant woman needs before she books.

Copy + Voice

03

The /
Reframe

Currently on site:

"Personalized Ayurvedic Guidance for Women in the second half of life. Lifelong Health Wisdom."

Keep the soul of it — but anchor it to the body. Your client wants the wisdom and the specific outcome: sleeping through the night, steady energy, digestion that works, ease through menopause. Name the ache, then lift it into your bigger philosophy.

You already have the empathy — "feeling good should not be a struggle" is perfect. Pair it with the specific woman and the specific result, and the right person feels both seen and understood in five seconds.

The test for every line on the page: could a woman forward it to a friend and say, "this is exactly what I've been feeling"? Your pain-point copy already passes. Let the outcome copy pass too.

Offer State — Current

You actually have a full funnel — a Dosha Quiz, recipes, a blog, a podcast, a free Clarity Call, and the Vitality Circle cohort. The problem isn't missing offers; it's that the homepage shows them all at once with equal weight, so a visitor can't tell where to start or what the paid path even is.

1

Entry

Dosha Quiz + Clarity Call

Your top-of-funnel is already strong. Make the Dosha Quiz THE front door — it segments and gives instant personalized value — leading to a free Clarity Call. One clear first step instead of five.

2

Core

Vitality Circle

Your signature cohort — the heart of the business. Give it its own clear story on the homepage: who it's for, what changes, the next cohort date and investment. Right now it competes with the freebies instead of standing out.

3

Premium

1:1 · Cleanses · Retreats

Your meta description mentions cleanses and retreats — but they're invisible on the homepage. Name the premium tier for women ready for deeper, personalized work. ⚑ NEEDS MANUAL INPUT: current 1:1 / retreat pricing

Operations

05

Back-End /
Infrastructure

  • ✓Platform: Kajabi — courses, email, payments, and community in one. A solid all-in-one foundation for a solo practice.
  • ✓Scheduling: Acuity is wired up for the Clarity Call. Frictionless booking is already in place.
  • ✓Owned audience: An active Facebook group, Instagram, YouTube, and an email list. You own the relationship, not just rent attention.
  • △Email capture: The signup sits at the footer. Move the Dosha Quiz / list opt-in up front and make it the primary capture.
  • △Funnel map: Several landing pages (Open House, Signature Program, Free Trial) with no single journey. Map it so every asset points somewhere on purpose.
  • △Proof system: No workflow collecting or displaying student results. Start one — it feeds every page.

Kajabi — lean into automations

You already have it. Use its email sequences and pipelines to wire Quiz → nurture → Clarity Call → Vitality Circle as one automated journey, instead of separate disconnected pages.

A testimonial / results workflow

Even a simple form plus a Kajabi section: collect student wins (energy, sleep, digestion, menopause relief) and display them. Ayurveda sells the invisible — proof is what makes it real to a newcomer.

The Dosha Quiz as segmentation

Tag quiz-takers by dosha and send dosha-specific nurture emails. You already built the quiz; connecting it to the email flow turns a fun tool into a personalized funnel.

one clear path from curious to committed.

The Math

06

Income /
Architecture

Current Pricing

Not Public ⚑ (Typical For Cohorts)

Primary Revenue

Vitality Circle Cohorts

Recurring Potential

Cohorts + Community / Membership

Secondary Streams

1:1 · Cleanses · Retreats · Digital

Cohort programs live and die by the size and warmth of the list feeding them. Your content engine — quiz, podcast, recipes, group — is the top of that funnel, and the money is in the sequence between a free Dosha Quiz and a full Vitality Circle seat. You don't need more traffic first; you need the path between what you already have to be one smooth line.

Fill the list first. The cohort follows.

07

Marketing

Content + Visibility

What's Working

  • ✓A genuine content engine — blog, recipes, Dosha Quiz, podcast, YouTube. Most coaches never build this much.
  • ✓An active Facebook group, Instagram, and email list — an owned audience you don't have to rent.
  • ✓The Dosha Quiz is a near-perfect lead magnet: it segments your audience AND gives instant, personalized value.

You already produce more content than you're monetizing. The win isn't making more — it's connecting what exists into one path that ends at Vitality Circle.

What Needs Building

  • △Proof: No testimonials or student results on the homepage. The single biggest trust gap for invisible, internal work.
  • △One primary CTA: Clarity Call, Open House, Free Trial, and email all compete. Choose one hero action and make the rest secondary.
  • △SEO title: "Work with Hannah and embrace your true Heartfelt Wellbeing" is brand-first. Lead with the search terms your meta description already nails — Ayurveda for women over 40, menopause.
  • △Homepage repetition: the hero is duplicated. Trim to one clean statement.

You have the niche, the content engine, and a signature program most coaches would envy. This isn't a substance problem.

It's a focus problem — too many paths, too little proof. Narrow to one, make the transformation visible, and the funnel you already built starts converting.

rX

Do these 6 things. In this order:

1

De-duplicate and simplify the homepage

The hero content repeats. Cut it to one clean above-the-fold: the concrete outcome named, one warm line of empathy, and a single primary CTA — Book Your Clarity Call. Everything else moves below.

2

Choose one primary path and sequence the rest

Make the journey explicit: Dosha Quiz → email nurture → Clarity Call → Vitality Circle. The Open House and Free Trial become supporting steps inside that path, not competing buttons on the homepage.

3

Add proof, front and center

Collect and display 3–6 student results — energy, sleep, digestion, menopause relief — plus Hannah's credentials and training. This is the missing trust layer that turns a hesitant reader into a Clarity Call booking.

4

Give Vitality Circle its own clear story

Who it's for, the transformation, the format, the next cohort date and investment range — in one dedicated, legible section. Right now your signature program is buried among the freebies.

5

Rewrite the SEO title and homepage H1

Keep the heart, add the search terms. Your meta description already ranks for "Ayurveda for women over 40" and "yoga for menopause" — put those in the title tag and H1 so search sends you the exact woman you serve.

6

Surface the offer ladder and premium tier

Make the free → Vitality Circle → 1:1 / cleanses / retreats path visible, so a woman who's ready to invest deeply can actually find the deeper work instead of only seeing the free layer.

You've built the wisdom and the audience.

the path just needs clearing.

Name the outcome. Show the proof.

xoxo

Heartfelt Wellbeing is ready.
